change: Selected Q4_K T16 dual WMMA prefill computes the two 16-column halves sequentially with one FP32 accumulator and retains per-lane output-column bounds checks before stores.

unsafe_intermediate_gate_summaries.note: Intermediate measurements were superseded because the first rewrite lacked the original per-lane store bounds guard for non-multiple-of-16 output widths.

prompt_verifier.evidence: Generated IDs stable on 512/128 and 4K/128 gate; focused selected-WMMA CPU-reference tests pass; no torch/llama.cpp hot-path changes; tracked peak unchanged at 21.334858 GiB; prefill improved strongly while decode gate movement is within run noise.

notes: The safe half-seq gate supersedes two intermediate measurements from the first half-seq rewrite because the first rewrite did not preserve the original per-lane store guard for non-multiple-of-16 output widths.
The selected Q4_K WMMA prefill bucket fell from 800.455 ms to 454.370 ms on the 4K/16 trace; the kernel still uses 256 VGPR and has 160 B private segment, so further live-state work may still help.

Decode primary metric is effectively flat versus selected-down-lb2 (-0.005%) and is not a decode promotion; the profiled 16-token decode window is noisy and measured 143.418 ms vs 138.513 ms baseline.
128K/128 on GPU1 failed at session construction with HIP OOM; do not mark final long-context promotion complete until a W7900 fallback or memory-policy gate is recorded.
